programme.title: The Texan
programme.desc: Wylie Ames, a big gruff widower, has been corresponding with a lonely woman whose personal ad he saw in an out-of town paper.

programme.title: The Texan
programme.desc: A crooked gambler with a Kentucky-born thoroughbred prods a drunken rancher into betting his entire spread and all the money he has in the bank on a horse race.

programme.title: State Plate
programme.desc: State Plate - Illinois: Did you know ice cream sundae was given its name in Evanston, Illinois? Well, this week, singer and entertainer Taylor Hicks enjoys a few scoops of the creamy confection as he tastes his way across Illinois looking to discover the state's most emblematic foods. Come along as he visits the cornfields that produce the official state snack: popcorn. He also learns how to bake the perfect bread for the famous Italian beef sandwich, and he heads to the windy city to get the low down on deep-dish pizza and the Chicago-style hot dog. To top it all off, Taylor visits the birthplace of the ice cream sundae. It's a plateful of Illinois cuisine filled with the stories and legends behind the state's tastiest foods.

programme.title: The Cowboy Way
programme.desc: The Cowboy Way - Friends 'Til the End: While visiting family in Mississippi, Cody secures prize cattle for his specialty sale, but one small mistake puts the deal in jeopardy. In anticipation of their move north, Bubba and Kaley visit their favorite places around town and take a trip down memory lane. Bubba breaks the news to his mother about the organic farm. After he catches her up on his current situation with the other cowboys, she encourages him to make things right. Booger heads to Austin, where he learns how to work with English-style horses and receives an offer to train horses for the entire summer. Booger returns home and gets a big surprise when Jaclyn introduces him to their newest family member. Cody reaches out to Booger and Bubba to get them all together so they can make amends and work out their differences. The cowboys consider taking a big job in Texas and working together again.

programme.title: Wild West Chronicles
programme.desc: Wild West Chronicles - Elfego Baca & the Frisco Shootout: Just days on the job and brandishing a homemade badge, nineteen-year-old Deputy Elfego Baca's courage is immediately put to the test. After arresting a drunken cowboy for shooting up a saloon, Baca faces down the cowboy's friends, who are hired guns for a wealthy rancher. He wins the first skirmish, but the cowboys return with reinforcements and the stage is set for the biggest and longest gunfight in Old West history. Baca seeks shelter in a small adobe house and makes his stand against a small army of men who shoot up the house with thousands of rounds of ammunition over the course of 33 hours. Years later, legendary gunfighter-turned-newspaperman Bat Masterson gets the real story of what comes to be known as the Frisco Shootout from the former sheriff who deputized Baca just prior to the legendary gun battle.

programme.title: Wild West Chronicles
programme.desc: Wild West Chronicles - The Hunt for Outlaw Bill Doolin: When notorious outlaw Bill Doolin escapes from jail, the U.S. Marshals Service springs into action to end his four-year crime spree. Known as the “Three Guardsmen,” Deputy U.S. Marshals Heck Thomas, Bill Tilghman, and Chris Madsen have a reputation for always getting their man, but they know it won't be easy with Doolin. Doolin is no ordinary outlaw: as a former member of The Dalton Gang and the mastermind of The Wild Bunch, he has successfully robbed banks, trains, and stagecoaches. After months of tracking Doolin, Heck Thomas is so close he barely stops to eat or sleep. When Thomas and his posse finally corner Doolin, he refuses to surrender, and gunfire is the only way out. Some years later, Bat Masterson, former-gunfighter-turned-newspaperman, catches up with Bill Tilghman to hear the real story behind the hunt for the outlaw Bill Doolin.

programme.title: Into The Wild Frontier
programme.desc: Into The Wild Frontier - Lewis & Clark: Captains of Discovery: Under orders from President Jefferson to explore America's vast unknown frontier, Meriwether Lewis and William Clark lead an expedition up the Missouri River in an attempt to reach the Pacific Ocean...a journey no American has made before, and one that may not even be possible. Leaving Fort Mandan after their first winter on the frontier, the Corps of Discovery enters completely uncharted territory. But as the waterways they travel become too shallow for boats, Lewis and Clark desperately seek the Shoshone tribe and their horses. To communicate with the elusive tribe, they rely on Sacagawea, a 16-year-old Shoshone woman, who accompanies the expedition with her newborn son. Lewis and Clark hope Sacagawea will help them trade for the Shoshone's horses, which will provide the only means of crossing the forbidding Rocky Mountains before winter returns.

programme.title: Ultimate Cowboy Showdown
programme.desc: Ultimate Cowboy Showdown - Tipping the Scales: The cowboys play Tic Tac Toe-with cows! Team Challenge: The team that sorts a herd with a total weight closest to 15,000 pounds wins.

programme.title: Wagon Train
programme.desc: Wagon Train - Alias Bill Hawks: Bill Hawks rides into a prairie town looking for his old Indian friend, but soon realizes he is not welcome. He discovers that the townspeople are drilling a well on his friend's land and won't give him any information on his whereabouts.

programme.title: Into The Wild Frontier
programme.desc: Into The Wild Frontier - William Bartram: America's First Great Naturalist: Naturalist William Bartram explores the unmapped regions of the South to document the native fauna and flora. After surviving many encounters with Native Americans and wildlife, it's an invisible virus that may finally end the journey and his life.

programme.desc: In this episode Blippi enjoys a day out Go Karting! Blippi learns all about the Go Karts from the engine to the wheels and goes for a ride alongside his friends.

programme.title: Blippi
programme.sub-title: Let's discover new things with Blippi
programme.desc: Make music with Blippi in this episode on percussion.

programme.title: Caillou
programme.sub-title: Dancing at Grandma's // Mystery Valentine // Play Ball! // Dogsled Ahead!
programme.desc: It is a rainy day and Caillou is disappointed because he will not be able to go play at the park with Grandma. // IIt's Valentine's day and Caillou is invited to a Valentine's party at his best friend Leo's house. // When Dad's baseball team, the Tigers, are playing a game at the park, Caillou, Rosie and Mom, go to cheer him on. // Caillou and his family are heading to the tobogganing hill!

programme.title: Caillou
programme.sub-title: Caillou's Sleepover Guest // Games in the Park // The Sugar Shack // Winter Mystery // Caillou's Snow Day
programme.desc: Leo is spending the night at Caillou's. // Caillou is excited when Miss Martin takes him and his friends to the park for some special activities. // When Caillou's family makes a springtime visit to Jonas' ranch, he takes them on a sleigh ride. // The twins, Jason and Jeffrey, have come to play with Caillou in the snow. // When Caillou, Rosie and Mom head to the big park, Caillou sees André.

programme.title: Caillou
programme.sub-title: Caillou's Favorite Plate // Fun in the Mud // All Aboard! // Gilbert's House // I Scream for Ice Cream!
programme.desc: Grandma has given Caillou a special spaceship plate. // It's raining outside but there is no stopping Caillou! // After finding a photograph of a four-year-old Dad with a model train set, Caillou and Dad decide to look for it. // When Caillou spots a dog house outside he gets the idea that Gilbert might like a house of his own. // Caillou, Rosie and Mom are having fun at the park when the ice-cream man comes around.

programme.title: Caillou
programme.sub-title: Super Caillou! // Trip On The Subway // Caillou's Race // As Good As New // I, Robot
programme.desc: Caillou just loves his Superbear storybook. // Caillou learns to deal with a change in plans, when Daddy has to use the car. // Caillou is playing a board game with Mom and he gets a little upset. // Caillou learns about 'recycling' -- literally! // When Sarah shows Caillou and Leo her new remote controlled robot, the boys get a great idea to be robots too!

programme.title: Caillou
programme.sub-title: Caillou's Water Park // Gone Fishing // The Water Goes 'Round // Caillou's Test Drive // A Very Good Swimmer!
programme.desc: Caillou learns that the best fun is made up and not bought and paid for. // Caillou learns about patience when he goes fishing with Daddy and Grandpa at the pier. // Grandma is watching Caillou for the day, and he wants her to play trucks with him. // When their car breaks down, the family goes shopping for a new one. // Caillou gets an introduction to what it means to be handicapped, when, at the pool, he meets Tammy.

programme.title: Caillou
programme.sub-title: Caillou's Horn // The Mighty Oak // Get Well Mr. Hinkle // The Big Boat
programme.desc: When Sarah gives Caillou an old toy horn, Caillou wants to blow it everywhere. // When Mr. Hinkle has a tree cut down, Caillou is afraid his swing tree is next! // When Caillou dresses up like a mailman to deliver Mr. Hinkle's misdirected mail, he finds out Mr. Hinkle is sick. // Caillou and family go on a trip on Helen's boat, an old friend of Mom's.

programme.title: Caillou
programme.sub-title: Something For Everyone // Skating Lessons // Caillou Becomes A Waiter // Sticking To It! // New Clothes
programme.desc: When the park is closed for the day, Caillou's friends come over to his house to play. // When Mom drives Sarah to her skating lesson, Caillou decides he wants to take skating lessons too! // When Mom and Dad are getting ready to go out to a restaurant, Caillou doesn't understand why he can't go too! // When Caillou accidentally breaks one of his toys because his room is a mess, Mom suggests a way to make cleaning up fun. // When Caillou dresses himself, he learns it's okay to change from your favourite shorts when you find out it's cold outside.

programme.title: Caillou
programme.sub-title: Where I Live // A Good Friend // Just Like Me // Just In Time // Mr. Fixit
programme.desc: When an invitation for Clementine's party accidentally ends up at Sarah's, Caillou learns the differences between the addresses of the two homes. // When Leo forgets his brand new toy dinosaur in the park, Daddy and Caillou go looking for it. // When Clementine tells Caillou how much she likes her older brother, Billy, to teach her things, Caillou sets out to teach something to Rosie. // When Caillou's dawdling causes he, Mom and Rosie to be late going to the park, they end up missing Leo. // When the washing machine needs fixing, Caillou ends up helping Bob the repairman.

programme.title: Caillou
programme.sub-title: Where's Gilbert? // Where I Saw It Last // Lost In The Jungle
programme.desc: Gilbert has gone missing and Caillou is very concerned. // After a day in the park and a visit to an ice cream parlour with Jeffrey and Jason, Caillou realizes that he has misplaced his new toy car. // Caillou learns all about the jungle at playschool.

programme.title: Caillou
programme.sub-title: Everyone's Best // Stronger Every Day // No More Training Wheels
programme.desc: When Clementine shows up with a swimming ribbon on her chest, Caillou really wants to have one too. // When a new, longer set of monkey bars is installed at the park, Caillou is disappointed. // With training wheels on his bike, Caillou is having trouble keeping up with Sarah.

programme.title: Caillou

programme.desc: When a white rabbit eats a bunch of carrots set aside for a picnic lunch, Caillou and Rosie go looking him. // When Caillou visits Clementine, he is impressed to see Billy practicing on a Tuba. // Caillou and his family are leaving for a picnic in the country but the ride feels very long for Caillou. // On a winter evening, a strange sound grabs Caillou's attention. // Caillou goes up to the attic with Mom and finds a box containing a teddy bear and a music box.

programme.title: Caillou

programme.desc: Monster Mania hits town when there is a sighting of the Pontypandyness Monster. Norman, determined to get the first sighting of the monster, takes Joe's Submaster 2000 without asking. Now trapped at the bottom of the lake, Sam and Penny must...

programme.title: Fireman Sam
programme.desc: Strong winds bring telegraph poles down and take the children's kite up - but a little higher than Sarah and James intended! Sam comes to the rescue. // On a very hot day, Sarah and James are picking potatoes for Bella. Sam wonders if he can...

programme.title: Fireman Sam
programme.desc: Saturday's are busy enough for Bella without having to deal with chip pan fires and lost cats. // Norman is up to his tricks as usual, but this time he plays one too many.

programme.title: Fireman Sam
programme.desc: Trevor's bus nearly puts an end to Sarah and James' camping trip with Uncle Sam. // T.V stardom comes to Station Officer Steele and everyone in Pontypandy in tuning in.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: On the Go: Riding, Sailing and Soaring
programme.desc: As little ones become increasingly mobile, their fascination with all things that move intensifies too. On the Go: Riding, Sailing and Soaring exposes babies to the different ways people travel -- on land, by sea, and in the air -- while it presents them with captivating real-world images, funny puppet shows, and beautiful classical music by Beethoven, Haydn, Mozart and Rossini.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: As little ones grow, their innate sense of wonder grows too, creating an occasion for celebration with each new discovery. Baby Galileo Discovering the Sky introduces them to that remarkable canopy above us: the sun and moon, fluffy clouds, shimmering stars, colorful planets and whirling galaxies.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: With the Dolittle, and other Baby programs under their belts, the tots who follow Julie Aigner-Clark's Baby Einstein series will sidle up to Baby Newton expecting a similar exploration of building blocks to brilliance, but this time there's a wrinkle or two. Animation makes its first appearance, and parents steeped in keep-it-simple culture may object to the springy "I Know My Shapes" song, a departure from classical-only video predecessors.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby Wordsworth: First Words Around the House
programme.desc: As their communication skills continue to develop, little ones begin to recognize common words and may point to objects or pictures when these items are named. Baby Wordsworth playfully taps into your little one's natural curiosity and introduces 30 words from around the home -- both spoken and in sign language. This engaging program presents little ones with familiar objects in the kitchen, playroom, yard and more -- from the living room window to their favorite teddy bear.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Meet fascinating creatures from across the globe and learn to run, hop, and crawl like your favorite animal friends. Dance and sing along to discover animal names and the native habitats of the world's most unique creatures. See real-life images of lions and zebras on the African savannah, then learn animal names through repetition and narration, or simply watch your favorite animals play!

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby MacDonald: A Day On the Farm
programme.desc: As little ones' curiosity about the world blossoms, you can help sow the seeds of discovery with Baby MacDonald: A Day on the Farm. Featuring live-action images, nursery rhymes and fun-filled puppet shows, this playful, interactive program introduces little ones to the exciting sights and sounds of a real working farm.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby's First Moves: Get Up and Go
programme.desc: Within the first year of babies' lives, their physical development begins to kick into high gear as they explore ways to physically interact with the world around them. BABY'S FIRST MOVES is a joyful celebration of their movement milestones -- including first steps, clapping, jumping, dancing and more.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Learn all about music and rhythm in the world around us! Discover classical music and learn instrument names through narration and repetition. Inspire sweet dreams with gentle melodies and a comforting bedtime story. Learn about classical music from Beethoven, Bach, and Mozart. Sing and dance along to classic rhythmic songs like The Wheels on the Bus and see real-life images of instruments.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Within a baby's first year of life, new experiences can transform what might otherwise seem to be ordinary events into exciting opportunities for imaginative play. Baby Neptune exposes little ones to the wonders of water in their world -- whether they're stomping in the rain, splashing in the bathtub, playing "catch me if you can" with the tide on the beach, or "meeting" the fascinating creatures that live in rivers, lakes and oceans.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Baby Mozart is an introduction to Mozart's beautiful music, which has been proven to have positive affects on people of all ages, especially babies. Images of brightly colored toys and known objects aid in the audio presentation of child-friendly arrangements and sound effects.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby's Favorite Places: First Words Around Town
programme.desc: As babies turn one year old, they continue to develop their communication skills. Meanwhile, with you as their guide, they also begin to explore the world beyond their front door. Baby's Favorite Places presents them with a playful introduction to 20 words for the names of familiar locations from the market, to the library, to the local park.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies dance an up and down dance. They watch some children painting pictures and patterns on to eggs. In Teletubbyland, Laa-Laa dances with her ball until the magical tree appears.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky says "Eh-oh". The Teletubbies and some children watch Tamzin Griffin pretend to be the Funny Lady and tell the story of the naughty hat. The Teletubbies are very happy when they find their favourite things to play with.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: It's time for the Teletubbies to do their exercises. A voice trumpet rises and the Teletubbies stretch up and down to it's instructions.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa run into Home Hill to say 'Eh-oh', but where 's Po? Some children help their dad to wash his car. They get very wet and soapy.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises and teaches the Teletubbies a nursery rhyme. Then they watch Becky go for a ride on a Tram. Po rides her scooter too fast and knocks the other Tubbies over.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies pop up from behind a hill to watch Tamzin Griffin pretend to be Funny Lady and tell the story of the naughty sausage. Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Po go for a walk, so Laa-Laa decides to dance a secret dance.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Two children are making Christmas crackers for each other. Dipsy is very pleased to find his present is a cracker. He looks for someone to pull the cracker. It makes a lovely pink bang and the Teletubbies laugh when they fall over.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies leave their favourite things to watch some boys spinning some spinning tops to celebrate the Hanukah festival. Tinky Winky wears the skirt on his head to do a very special round and round dance.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies and some children watch Tamzin Griffin pretend to be Funny Lady and tell the story of the Naughty Pig. Dipsy's hat keeps blowing away and he has to chase it all over Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet appears and the Teletubbies dance, before watching two girls do some Flamenco dancing with their teacher. It's time for the Teletubbies to wash their feet. They love using their Tubby Sponges.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies see some children visit a level crossing and watch a train pass by. The Teletubbies play their Ready, Steady, Go game.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch Sarah and Mark give their sheep a winter-feed. In Teletubbyland, the Teletubbies cannot decide whether to have Tubby Custard or Tubby Toast.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies want to go to sleep, but Tinky Winky finds Po already asleep in his bed. So he goes to look for somewhere else to sleep.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ learns that taking care of a pet is more than just having fun. Baby Bop's teddy bear gets torn, prompting a vet visit to Barney and Rif.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: In winter, Baby Bop realizes that winter can still be a time for fun. In summer, BJ learns of fun activities to do and the importance of relaxation.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: On Barney's birthday, Riff worries over his hand-made gift. Baby Bop learns rhythm with help from friends, just in time for her marching band debut.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop learns rules make games fun with Barney's help. BJ hurts his toe while playing outside and discovers the wonderful world of books.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.sub-title: Big as Barney - No, No, No
programme.desc: When Ryan tries to be just like Barney, he has a hard time filling Barney's shoes. Every time Baby Bop turns around, someone is telling her no!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Ryan is tempted to break the rules in a contest. When Riff meets a friend who can't hear well, Riff wonders if he would still like music.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney and his friends attend a big Halloween party and go trick-or-treating.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ feels left out when the kids have a Dog Show to show off their pets. The kids fight over wanting to do the same thing.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.sub-title: Dream Big - Thats What A Mommy Is
programme.desc: Riff daydreams of flying and builds his very own rocket ship and fly to Mars. Baby Bop is curious as to what makes somebody a mommy.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bops convinced her blanket is shrinking. When Riff takes a bite of ice cream, he realizes has a very sore tooth.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Marcos accidentally breaks an art show project that Melanie has made. Meanwhile, Myra, has decided she doesn't like her name.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.sub-title: The Magic Caboose - BJ the Great
programme.desc: Barney and friends take imaginary trips to different countries with the magical caboose. Then, BJ wants to be a magician without practicing.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.sub-title: Gift of the Dinos - A Visit to Santa
programme.desc: Barney and friends plan a Secret Santa game. Barney takes Melanie to Santa's workshop at the North Pole to deliver her wish list in person.

programme.title: Bob the Builder

programme.title: Bob the Builder

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Mr Bentley - Dogsitter / Travis Gets Lucky
programme.desc: Bob and the gang convert a house into two separate flats. / Farmer Pickles is having problems with mice.

programme.title: Bob the Builder

programme.title: Bob the Builder

programme.desc: Mr Bentley is organising a winter fair. / Bob installs an air conditioning unit at Mr Sabatini's restaurant.

programme.title: Bob the Builder

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Skip's Big Idea / Roley's Important Job
programme.desc: Bob, Scoop and Skip are installing a new Rockery. / Bob is converting a local house into a delicatessen.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: It's the team's first full day in Sunflower Valley. / The machines get to build their brand new shelter.

programme.title: Bob the Builder

programme.desc: Bob and the team are drilling a well at their homestead. / Scoop and Dizzy help Wendy with digging and mixing.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob builds a drying barn for Farmer Pickles sunflower farm. / The team build Farmer Pickles a new factory.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the team build a special eco-home. / The team are building Sunflower Valley's first geodesic dome.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Spud The Spanner / Bob's White Christmas
programme.desc: Spud decides being a Scarecrow is hard work! / It's Christmas Eve and the machines are very excited.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Mrs Broadbent's new 'granny' flat needs decorating. / Bob has two jobs to do today.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: From the BerryWorks to Strawberry's Café, everyone is excited about who will win this year's Spring Flower Festival! Berry Bitty City is decorated with colorful banners proclaiming the big event. Lemon works the hardest. For weeks she has watered her violets by day and sung to them at night, doing everything she can to win first prize. With the contest just a day away, Lemon is at her garden early.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry Shortcake's friends watch her with wide eyes as she everrrrr-so-gently squirts a layer of blueberry smoothie into a tall glass, trying to not disturb the five other multi-colored layers beneath it. It's her tenth attempt to create the world's first Rainbow Smoothie! She has even painted one wall of her café rainbow colors in honor of the soon-to-be new item on the menu. No one dares breathe for fear of spoiling the delicate touch of a master smoothie-squirter at work.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Blueberry ordered some Zippy Gro daisy seeds and planted them in her garden. She is surprised that they've sprouted in less than a day despite their descriptive moniker. Strawberry comes by to admire them and Blueberry is reminded that the new cookbook Strawberry ordered has arrived. Strawberry is excited to try out the new corn muffin recipe, as its level of difficulty will be a challenge for her. Strawberry follows the muffin recipe, mixes the ingredients, and double checks it.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.sub-title: The Berry Best You Can Bee
programme.desc: Strawberry is at Orange's store to mail a birthday card to a friend in Big Bitty City. Just after she drops the card into the mail slot, an ill postal carrier bee sneezes sending envelopes and post cards flying in all directions. The two girls help Jadeybug, a postal clerk, and Postmaster Bee gather up the strewn letters and the ill bee gets sent home from work.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: While rehearsing a song alone in her cafe, Strawberry is interrupted by the arrival of Jadeybug. The poor ladybug is stressed out because her twin cousins, Kadiebug and Sadiebug, left her a message that they are coming to visit for a whole week. Jadey is unable to host them because she's having her guest room repainted.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: While at Orange's general store to pick up a package, Berrykin Bloom chats with Orange about the pogostick he gave her to help her reach the upper shelves. She is very appreciative. He ends up getting a package of special berry seeds he ordered, not the vacation magazine Orange thought he came for. He explains how the seeds were a better purchase because of all the benefits to the Berryworks but now he can no longer afford a vacation.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: All of Berry Bitty City is gathered around to watch the last snowberrykin melt. As the last bit of snow forms a puddle, a cheer goes up, spring is here! It's now time for the annual BerryFest. But with Princess Berrykin out of town on business, who will be the BerryFest Princess and preside over the festival?! Everyone is abuzz with how to go about choosing the Princess and what her duties will be. Postmaster Bee declares the only fair way to go about it is to hold an election. Everyone agrees.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry opens her front door to a throng of Berrykins all cheering for the new BerryFest Princess. She is appreciative of their admiration but unsure of exactly what her duties entail. In response the Berrykins bombard her with her duties - major to miniscule. One Berrykin sums it up by telling her she's responsible for presiding over all the activities of the BerryFest to make it the best.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: All of Berry Bitty City is excitedly making their way through the berry batch in search of the elusive Wanderberry. It is to be the dessert at the BerryFest Feast. Everyone is milling about searching for it on their own when Blueberry points out that the rules say the BerryFest Princess is in charge of the search. The girls turn to her for direction and she points them where she thinks the berry might be.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.sub-title: Where, Oh, Where Has Our Blueberry Gone?
programme.desc: Blueberry has become obsessed with the mystery book detective heroine, Patti Persimmon. She decorates her store with Patti posters and rearranges her shelves to make room for all the Patti books she's ordered. Blueberry begins to neglect outings with her friends and even goes so far as to dress like her idol just to emulate her. She then starts looking for cases to solve around town.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: While in the midst of a cake decorating demonstration at the café, the girls are uncharacteristically interrupted by an excited Princess Berrykin. She announces the Berrykins have decided to revive the old tradition of the Berry Bitty Buggy Derby. While the girls enthusiastically recount the stories they've heard about it and ask the Princess questions, Blueberry, carrying enough books to block her view, comes through the door and unknowingly interrupts the Princess.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: The grove around Berry Bitty City is abuzz with activity. The Berrykins are using their sparkles to harvest berries, Berrykin Bloom is in his garden spraying flowers with a special solution to make them larger, and Raspberry is nearby gathering flower petals to make perfume. The wind starts to blow and picks up some of the sparkles. The sparkles float along the wind and get combined with some of Berrykin Bloom's solution.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: It's time again for the Dance-Off between the Big Bitty City Dance Team and the Berry Bitty City Dancers. Plum is stressed out and frazzled because she desperately wants to win. And the Big Bitty City Dancers are a force to be reckoned with. They are elegant and beautifully synchronized. Plum shows the girls a maze of dance steps that she's choreographed in the hopes of creating a winning piece. The problem is, the contest is only 1 week away!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.sub-title: Ginger Snap's No-Light Night of Fright
programme.desc: Strawberry & Friends go on an overnight campout, Ginger Snap doesn't want them to find out, she's afraid of the dark!

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: Twilight Sparkle tracks down Starlight Glimmer in the past and tries to find a way to stop her revenge plot.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: Twilight and her friends head to the Crystal Empire for the "Crystalling" of Cadence and Shining Armor's new baby.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: Starlight tries to pick up the pieces of the failed reunion with her old friend.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.sub-title: The Gift Of The Maud Pie
programme.desc: While Rarity has to help Pinkie Pie find the perfect gift for her sister, Maud - without Maud finding out.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: Zeb and Erebus nearly steal everyone's shadow before Molly's ride for help is a success.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: Sweet Stuff feels like she doesn't fit in anywhere, until she figures out a special riddle.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: The Unicorns disappear from Ponylnad, leaving only the clue "Tambelon" in a dream.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: The medieval city of Tambelon has returned from the Realm of Darkness.

programme.title: My Little Pony

programme.title: My Little Pony

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: When Starlight Glimmer starts becoming friends with a fellow formerly-bad pony, Twilight tries to stop her.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: Rainbow Dash finally becomes a Wonderbolt, but makes a disastrous first impression with the team.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: It's Hearth's Warming Eve and everypony is excited except for Starlight Glimmer.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.sub-title: The Fast and the Furriest / Disappearing Act

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: Twilight Sparkle faces a a foe unlike any other. / Discord is encouraged to join the Trail Trotters troop.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.sub-title: A Camping We Will Go / Campfire Stories
programme.desc: The Mane 6 go on camping trip. / The ponies entertain themselves by telling some good old-fashioned stories.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: The ponies teach Trixie the true meaning of friendship. / The ponies try to introduce Fluttershy to her hero.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.sub-title: The Fault In Our Cutie Marks
programme.desc: The Crusaders encounter a problem that might be impossible for them to solve.

programme.title: My Little Pony
programme.desc: Rarity opens her flagship store in Manehattan.

programme.title: The Berry Long Winter
programme.desc: On a beautiful winter's night, Blueberry reads the Baby Berrykins a story of what happened one winter in Berry Bitty City—a winter that almost wasn't. Just as everyone is ready for winter fun and fashions, a sudden warming melts the snow and ruins their plans. Improvising with snowmen made of shaved ice and cotton puffs, Strawberry and her friends play “Capture the Snowman's Carrot Nose.” The Berrykins want to join in the fun, so the girls play against them, with Strawberry refereeing. But it turns out the Berrykins' version of the game has different rules: they zoom around on little hang gliders made of leaves!

programme.title: Foods that are Flat
programme.desc: The Taste Buds get the skinny on theirfavourite flat foods. Lily and Matt make two mouthwatering kinds of mandarin orange crepes and discover that one of the flattest foods is also the most versatile. Avery visits an Ethiopian restaurant, where they skip the cutlery and try different toppings on the deliciously flat injera bread. Then Lily & Avery make a selection of slim and healthy sandwiches to find out which is their flat-out favourite.

programme.title: Foods that are Sweet
programme.desc: When Chillbot alerts Lily that her root beer float will have her floating in sugar, can she find a way to beat the heat with something that's not too sweet? Avery taps into the secrets of an authentic sugar shack, and finds out how oh- so-slow maple sap becomes oh-so-sweet maple syrup. Then Lily and Avery flip for their own fabulous flapjacks – and discover that fresh fruit is the perfect pancake companion. Sweet!

programme.title: Contact
programme.desc: Yusaku Fuiki, has become a celebrity in the aftermath of battling Hanoi. He continues to investigate the mysterious program he dubs "Ai." Meanwhile, SOL Technologies begins to look deeper into the identity of Playmaker.

programme.title: Pain and Gain
programme.desc: Go Onizuka, a popular duelist on Link VRAINS, accepts an offer from SOL to duel Playmaker. He wants to prove himself and he sees Playmaker as a powerful opponent. He lures Yusaku into a trap and a Speed Duel begins.

programme.title: Blippi's School Supply Scavenger Hunt
programme.desc: Blippi embarks on a fantastic back-to-school adventure!

programme.title: The Big Freeze
programme.desc: It's another windy winter day, and the postal bees are staying indoors—the wind is too strong for their berry bitty wings. “We don't know when the next mail will get through!” It's bad news for Blueberry, who has been anxiously awaiting the next installment of a Patty Persimmon Detective story. If only she could get to the post office in Berry Bitty Dale where her package is waiting! She gets a brainstorm. Cutting up the side of an old broken tennis racket, she attaches the pieces to her rain boots—instant ice skates! Fashionably bundled against the cold, and pulled by butterflies who sail on the wind like kites, she sail-skates down the frozen road, to the amazement of her friends.

programme.title: On Ice
programme.desc: It appears winter is finally coming to an end and Raspberry is planning a big fashion show to display her spring line. Her vision is to have the girls model her fashions in a parade past the town fountain. What she didn't realize is that the Berrykins are turning the fountain into a big water show. Every time the girls come to rehearse, they must comically dodge jets of fruit juice from the out-of-control fountain—the Berrykins are still working out the bugs. Strawberry reminds them that this is a public area that should be shared, and suggests they work out a schedule.

programme.title: Beware Of The Wolf -Winx Club
programme.desc: Before it is too late, Miele comes to help the fairies, using the antidote to replace the fungi with beautiful magic orchids. The Winx thank Flora's sisters , but now they longer have the antidote to stop the fungi's threat. However, noticing that nature is spontaneously reacting, Flora gets the idea of helping nature itself with her own Butterflix special spell. Doing so, Flora not only cancel the fungi's mutation, but also rescues all the MagiWolves, magically bonding with Amarok.

programme.title: Back To The Middle Ages
programme.desc: After a few attempts to make Squonk and Amarok become friends, the Winx use their Stones of Memories to travel back to Medieval Italy, where a very special animal species, called Quillcat, used to live. This species of Fairy Animals could interest Brafilius, so the Winx, to anticipate his moves, want to find the animal before him. However, they meet a group of minstrels who, in exchange for their help, ask the fairies a favour, that is to play the role of fairies in a play…

programme.title: No Blueberry Is an Island
programme.desc: With the summer almost over, Blueberry overhears Strawberry talking about a wonderful tropical island and believes her friend is going to surprise them all with a much needed vacation! Blueberry makes the other girls promise to keep the secret until Strawberry reveals the big surprise. Of course, even though they've promised to keep the secret, they can't help but try and suss out what the island's like from every little thing Strawberry does, so they can prepare accordingly—they see Strawberry wearing a diving mask and snorkel in the cafe, and race to the lake to practice scuba diving, not knowing she was only slicing onions.

programme.title: Where the Berry Breeze Blows
programme.desc: It's the last week of summer and Berry Bitty City is hopping with activity as the girls prepare for their tropical island vacation at Berry Breezy Bay Resort. But just as they're about to leave, bad news: the resort is closed for the season due to berry breezy weather. The girls are disappointed, but Orange isn't giving up. She says if they can't go to the tropical island, maybe they can bring the tropical island here! She insists they can build a copy of “Berry Breezy Bay” resort on Berry Bitty Lake, a duplicate down to the last detail.

programme.title: Foods that Fuel
programme.desc: Fill'er up! When Avery needs to up his energy, he discovers that sugary sports drinks can't beat the boost he gets from his home made Power Smoothies and Awesome Fuel Bars. Lily hits the ice with an Olympic gold-medal hockey player and finds out what happens when you try to run on empty. Will the right snack get her back on track? Lily and Avery head back to the kitchenfor a batch of the ultimate, high- octane meal: a new take on traditional spaghetti & meatballs.

programme.title: Foods that Crunch
programme.desc: It's crunch time! Lily fights soggy cereal witha crunchy batch of her very own granola and learns the history behind crunchy breakfast cereals with History Buff. Avery visits an apple orchard, where he meets an apple expert and learns how apples get from the tree to your local grocery store. Avery and Lily team up to make homemade oven-baked chips, and use their Crunch-o-meter to find out which kind of healthy chip is the King of Crunch!

programme.title: I Want Cake!
programme.desc: Sid and his friends discover that only eating cake would make their bodies feel terrible. Then they explore and learn that the best way to stay healthy is by eating nutritious food.

programme.title: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5… Numberblocks!
programme.desc: The animated adventures of friends who can always count on each other Find out what happens when numbers come to life and how they really work - and play - with the Numberblocks. Season 3, episodes 9 - 12.

programme.title: Happy the Hoglet
programme.desc: In Happy's world of Sunny River Community Garden, big feelings are as natural as the carrots growing in raised beds. All living things here coexist in perfect balance…with a lot of funny fun funny! Season 1, episodes 14-16.

programme.title: Happy the Hoglet
programme.desc: In Happy's world of Sunny River Community Garden, big feelings are as natural as the carrots growing in raised beds. All living things here coexist in perfect balance…with a lot of funny fun funny! Season 1, episodes 17-19.

programme.title: Interstellar Ella
programme.desc: In the year 3021, on a space station somewhere between Mars and Jupiter in the Milky Way, 8-year-old Ella Ryder sets out with her friends on exciting adventures of discovery. Season 1, episodes 49 & 50.

programme.title: Interstellar Ella
programme.desc: Ella & friends enjoy exciting adventures in space, where there is lots to explore! Season 1, episodes 51&52.

programme.title: Pop Paper City
programme.desc: Pop Paper City - Frightful Friend: On today's poptastic adventure, Plom is having a spooky sleepover! But when Zip gets scared and decides to stay home, Plom crafts him a friendly monster. Can Plom show Zip that sometimes scary things can be fun too?

programme.title: Shaun The Sheep
programme.desc: Shaun The Sheep - Little Sheep of Horrors: Intrigued by a horror film on TV, Timmy wanders into the farmhouse late one night. Shaun's rescue mission convinces the Farmer that the place is haunted.

programme.title: Interstellar Ella
programme.desc: Interstellar Ella - Ghost Story: Slippy's ghost detector goggles come in handy when odd events occur. Ella's skeptical, but Maggie's gibberish, levitating objects, and Glitch floating baffle them. Ella seeks an explanation, and Slippy's goggles solve the mystery a magnetic star!

programme.title: Shaun The Sheep
programme.desc: Shaun The Sheep - Bitzer from the Black Lagoon: While trying to retrieve his ball, Bitzer falls into a muddy pond. When Shaun sees a big, blobby, shambling “thing” squelching across the field he's convinced they are being attacked by a swamp monster... Run!

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: After a summer of competing in their own sporting extravaganza, Shaun and the Flock return, along with Shaun's best mate, the ever faithful Bitzer, the Farmer, and all the Mossy Bottom Farm favourites. Season 3, episodes 18 - 20.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Shaun, the world's most famous and favourite sheep, is back for more laugh out loud adventures on Mossy Bottom Farm. Season 4, episodes 1 - 3.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Shaun, the world's most famous and favourite sheep, is back for more laugh out loud adventures on Mossy Bottom Farm. Season 4, episodes 10 - 12.

programme.desc: Follow the adventures of ArachnoFly, as interpreted by ArachnoFly superfan Lloyd – star of Lloyd of the Flies – which follows the half-fly half-spider comicbook hero as he patrols Cereal City. Season 1, Episodes 11-16.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Behind the Scenes // Paperdoll in Peril
programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried about the play planned for tonight. There are no costumes ready for the villagers so it's hard to figure out which roles they're playing. Rainbow Ruby transforms into a costume designer and designs costumes for her friends. // It is very windy in Rainbow village and Paige, a paper doll, is flying to wherever the wind takes her. She is so light that she is blown away by the wind. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a fire fighter and rescue Paige.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Singing in the Rain // Mail Carrier Mayhem
programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried that the villagers are all in low spirits. Ruby thinks of fun games to play but the villagers say that they have already played all of the games too many times. Rainbow Ruby transforms into a singer. // Ling Ling is worried because of the "Secret Present Swapping Party" that Thunderbell is preparing. Thunderbell forgot to send out the invitations. Rainbow Ruby turns into a mail carrier and delivers the invitations.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Going Golfing // Case of the Stolen Spectacles
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that a Rainbow Ball Tournament is about to start soon but he is worried because the field is covered with pokey pods. Thunderbell, Ellie, and Gina are trying to pick them up and clear the field but it is not easy since the pokey pods are so spikey. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that he lost his glasses. Then Ellie tells her she lost her needles, Gina her hat, and Thunderbell his wheels. Who could have taken away all these things?

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Up in the Air // AdDRESSing the Problem
programme.desc: Ling Ling is waiting for Rainbow Ruby, looking worried. It turns out that Mr. Sloth's best friend Chirpee is depressed. Chirpee wants to fly high but is struggling due to his small wings and lack of means to learn how to fly. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that the dress that the paper doll Paige was planning to wear to Kiki's dance party is torn. Paige very much wants to go to the party but she cannot go without a dress.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Kiki wants to become a movie actress but the villagers have no idea how to direct a movie. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a movie director. She then assigns roles to the village friends. // Ling Ling rushes toward Rainbow Ruby to tell her that a busy villager mom asked him to take care of her three children but he just doesn't have time to look after them. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to transform into a babysitter and starts babysitting the children.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Wakey Wakey // Rules of the Road
programme.desc: Princess Kiki is asleep, UP IN THE AIR! According to the note that Kiki left behind, a princess falls under a spell from time to time and in order to break it, they need to play the music box nearby Kiki. But the music box is broken. // Rainbow Ruby and Choco discover cars moving in every direction. Ling Ling asks Rainbow Ruby for help and she uses her magic suitcase to become a police officer.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that today is Noodle Festival day in the village but there is a problem. The problem is that the noodle tree, which grows noodles, decided to take a year off and is not growing any noodles this year. // Ling Ling and the friends are worried about the discouraged Gina. Gina just announced that she will stop doing somersaults anymore because she accidentally hurt her friends while practicing.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: A Colorful Way to Cool Off // Seeing in Believing
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that Rainbow Village friends were playing around the Rainbow fountain to cool off from the heat but water suddenly stopped coming out of the fountain. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a plumber and starts fixing the fountain. // Kiki's face has turned purple because she ate magical Plumen berries. Kiki accidentally picked magical Plumen berries instead of the delicious rainbow berries she was trying to gather.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Bad Hair Day // Ice is Nice
programme.desc: Ellie's and Jessy's hair are a complete mess after Felicia accidentally causes some Rainbow fruit to fall on them. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a hairdresser. // It's winter in Rainbow Village. Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that last night a huge ice mountain appeared so everyone is enjoying an ice sculpture festival. But Jessy seems to have a toothache.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: The ground is all wet in Rainbow Village. What's going on? The friends tell Ruby that Jessy's tears have soaked the ground. Jessy accidentally ate Ellie's magic beans and became as huge as a giant. // Thunderbell is very sad because his best friend, Mr. Sloth, is angry at him. Rainbow Ruby thinks up the best way to apologize to Mr. Sloth, who loves performances. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a choreographer.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: How Does Your Garden Grow // Mystery Gifts
programme.desc: Kiki wanted to make a nice garden so she asked the Poppies to do some gardening but they planted the seeds everywhere in the garden, making an absolute mess. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a gardener.// Ling Ling is setting up a 'Thankfulness' tree at the center of the town square. He tells Rainbow Ruby that he is going to hold an event where the village friends can write notes to people that they are thankful to and hang them up on the tree.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Rainbow Village friends are on a train and they are concerned that the train won't stop. The engine is even smoking! Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a firefighter. // Paige is extremely upset. It turns out that she's been preparing an awesome fashion show but now it had to be cancelled because Gina accidentally spilled a bunch of fruit onto the stage.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: The Rainbow Wave // The Best Pet You Never Met
programme.desc: Rainbow Village friends are gathered together at the lake to surf on the Rainbow wave that only comes once a year and they need some help. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a lifeguard and helps her friends safely enjoy their surfing. // Thunderbell is looking extremely worried. It turns out that his pet fish has gone missing. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a detective and finds out Thunderbell's pet fish's whereabouts.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: A welcome party for Prince Frederick is taking place in Rainbow Village, but the guest of honour isn't having any fun at all. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a party planner. // The village is a total mess from the paint that spilled from up above. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a clean-up engineer and starts cleaning with her friends.

programme.title: Counting With Paula

programme.desc: The Junior Rangers journey to the Savannah to find a cheetah cub's mother.

programme.title: Leo The Wildlife Ranger
programme.desc: Ranger Rocky entrusts the Junior Rangers with a mission, return a mola to the ocean!

programme.title: Leo The Wildlife Ranger
programme.desc: The Junior Rangers travel to the rainforest to reunite a Malayan tiger cub with its mother.

programme.title: Leo The Wildlife Ranger
programme.desc: The Junior Rangers head to the prairie to find a suitable home for an eastern spotted skunk.

programme.title: Leo The Wildlife Ranger

programme.title: Leo The Wildlife Ranger
programme.desc: Kyle has fallen into a Wombat's burrow! Can the Junior Rangers find him in time?

programme.title: Leo The Wildlife Ranger
programme.desc: Kyle claims to have discovered a brand new animal species. Kylasaurus needs help!

programme.desc: Can the Junior Rangers outsmart a Kea and get their jeep keys back?

programme.title: Leo The Wildlife Ranger

programme.desc: The Junior Rangers set out to rescue Kyle's beloved toy kangaroo, Stella, from an actual Red Kangaroo.

programme.title: Leo The Wildlife Ranger
